title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in evaluation and governance rather than new model releases. The strongest updates focus on how to audit simulated behavior, test agent compliance with access boundaries, generate safety-critical scenarios for autonomous systems, and frame frontier AI governance in policy terms.

items.title: Will the Agent Recuse Itself? Measuring LLM-Agent Compliance with In-Band Access-Deny Signals
items.why_it_matters: Tests whether autonomous agents respect soft access-deny signals before stronger technical controls are standardized.

items.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
items.why_it_matters: Shows whether simulated user beliefs are robust or easily distorted by irrelevant context changes.

items.title: RiskFlow: Fast and Faithful Safety-Critical Traffic Scenario Generation
items.why_it_matters: Improves rare-event testing for autonomous systems, a core bottleneck in safety evaluation.

items.title: Risk Assessment of Autonomous Driving: Integrating Technical Failures, Ethical Dilemmas, and Policy Frameworks
items.why_it_matters: Combines technical, ethical, and regulatory risk lenses for a higher-stakes deployment domain.

items.title: A blueprint for democratic governance of frontier AI
items.why_it_matters: Offers a concrete governance proposal for frontier AI safety, resilience, and national-security oversight.

items.title: Benchmark Everything Everywhere All at Once
items.why_it_matters: Targets benchmark scalability and reuse, which affects how quickly model risks can be evaluated.

items.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
items.why_it_matters: Pre-registered testing helps separate real capability gains from prompt-formatting artifacts in evaluations.

items.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
items.why_it_matters: Addresses detection in mixed human-AI writing, a growing provenance and integrity challenge.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
